title: [FIRRTL][Dedup] Support deduplicating local inner symbol uses
body:
Consider:
```firrtl
FIRRTL version 3.0.0

circuit RWProbePortDedup: %[[
{
  "class": "firrtl.transforms.MustDeduplicateAnnotation",
  "modules": ["~RWProbePortDedup|A","~RWProbePortDedup|B"]
}
]]

  module A:
    input x : UInt<5>
    output p : RWProbe<UInt<5>>

    define p = rwprobe(x)

  module B:
    input x : UInt<5>
    output p : RWProbe<UInt<5>>

    define p = rwprobe(x)

  module RWProbePortDedup:
    input x : UInt<5>
    output p_a : RWProbe<UInt<5>>
    output p_b : RWProbe<UInt<5>>

    inst a of A
    connect a.x, x
    define p_a = a.p

    inst b of B
    connect b.x, x
    define p_b = b.p
```

Run with `firtool -dedup`, Dedup will be unable to deduplicate `A` and `B` because of the `RWProbeOp` operations differ in their target:

```
rwprobe_port_dedup.fir:3:1: error: module "B" not deduplicated with "A"
circuit RWProbePortDedup: %[[
^
rwprobe_port_dedup.fir:14:16: note: first operation has attribute 'target' with value #hw.innerNameRef<@A::@sym>
    define p = rwprobe(x)
               ^
rwprobe_port_dedup.fir:20:16: note: second operation has value #hw.innerNameRef<@B::@sym>
    define p = rwprobe(x)
               ^
```
